use_cases.name: Evaluate an LLM
use_cases.short: Run benchmarks, regression suites, or LLM-as-judge scoring against a model.
use_cases.description:
Score a model — public benchmarks (MMLU, HumanEval, ARC-AGI), task-specific
eval suites, A/B comparison, or LLM-as-judge pipelines. Pair with a
governance entry below if you need policy-gated regression.

use_cases.name: Red-team an AI system
use_cases.short: Adversarial probes, jailbreak suites, agentic attack simulators across chatbots, agents, and pipelines.
use_cases.description:
Find unsafe outputs before users do — prompt-injection, jailbreaks,
agentic exfiltration, prompt-leak, tool misuse, multi-turn coercion.
Run continuously in CI against any AI surface (chatbot, agent,
RAG pipeline, tool-using assistant); the best harnesses ship
taxonomies plus generators rather than a fixed bench.

use_cases.name: Monitor production agents
use_cases.short: Trace, log, score, alert — observability for LLM-powered systems.
use_cases.description:
Capture every prompt/response, attach evaluator scores, watch for
drift, route traces by tenant. Most are OTEL-compatible.

use_cases.name: Build a RAG agent
use_cases.short: Compose retrieval, reasoning, and tool-use into an agent.

use_cases.name: Serve LLM inference
use_cases.short: Stand up an OpenAI-compatible endpoint on your own hardware.
use_cases.description:
Self-host inference — vLLM/sglang/TGI for high-throughput GPU,
llama.cpp/ollama for laptops, BentoML/TensorRT-LLM for production.
Pair with a routing layer if you need provider failover.

use_cases.name: Route between providers
use_cases.short: One API, many models — failover, cost routing, A/B, tenant policy.
use_cases.description:
A gateway in front of OpenAI/Anthropic/local — for cost routing,
failover, tenant-keyed policy, rate-limit safety. Most also do
tracing and caching.

use_cases.name: Govern LLM policy
use_cases.short: Validators, guardrails, structured outputs, audit-log FSMs.
use_cases.description:
Enforce the five components — schema-validate outputs, gate
side-effects, redact PII, log the FSM. The strongest entries are
validator-first (Pydantic-style) rather than prompt-asks-please.

use_cases.name: Learn the stack
use_cases.short: Cookbooks, courses, from-scratch implementations, paper notebooks.
use_cases.description:
Curated learning paths — first-principles ("LLMs from scratch"),
official cookbooks (Anthropic / OpenAI / Mistral), and annotated
paper repos. Start here if you're new.

supply_chains.name: Build & ship an agent
supply_chains.description:
The canonical hosted-agent stack — self-host inference, route through
a gateway for failover and budget control, orchestrate with a graph
framework, gate releases with eval, observe in prod.

supply_chains.name: TDD an LLM feature
supply_chains.description:
Test-first for LLM features — write evals before the prompt, run them
in CI with the same rigour as unit tests, capture regressions
against the harness's own snapshot.

supply_chains.name: Red-team → harden
supply_chains.description:
Discover unsafe outputs first, then close them — adversarial probe →
structural-output guardrail → policy validator → continuous re-probe
in CI.

supply_chains.name: RAG end-to-end
supply_chains.description:
Document → retrieval → reasoning → grounded answer → eval. RAG
frameworks typically own steps 1–3; you bring the eval and trace.

supply_chains.name: Fine-tune → evaluate
supply_chains.description:
Post-training pipeline — supervised fine-tune, preference-optimise
(DPO/GRPO), score against held-out benchmarks, gate the release.

supply_chains.name: Multi-tenant LLM gateway
supply_chains.description:
Stand up an internal LLM platform — auth, per-tenant policy and
budget, audit trail, validator-enforced outputs, full traces.
